_comment: Espionage mission catalogue (§7.1, §15.5). Accrued intel points (EP) against a target alliance are spent on a mission picked from this table. Per-mission cost = intel_mission_cost × cost_multiplier/100 × (1 + EP_advantage/100); the EP-advantage curve and base interception live in constants.json and are computed in SimFacade. `effect` is the verb SimFacade._espionage_apply dispatches; each is a deterministic application against the most-populous valid target city (lowest id on a tie). Verbs: steal_tech (copy one technology the attacker lacks from the target); sabotage (halve a target city's stored production); destroy_building (remove the costliest non-Palace structure from a target city); destroy_project (cancel an in-progress endgame project, wiping its stored production); destroy_improvement (clear a tile improvement worked by a target city); steal_gold (transfer up to `amount` gold from the richest target member to the attacker); poison_water (remove 1 population from the largest target city of population >= 2); insert_culture (add `amount` of the attacker's cultural influence to a target city's tile, feeding §4.9 revolt pressure); incite_unhappiness (add a timed angry-citizen modifier of `amount` faces for `duration` turns to a target city); incite_revolt (tip the largest target city into disorder and start a `duration`-turn occupation-style revolt); switch_civic (force a target member into `duration` turns of governmental anarchy); switch_religion (strip a target member's state religion and force `duration` turns of anarchy); counterespionage (the attacker holds heightened interception of the target's missions for `duration` turns). Each mission is only offered/accepted when its target gate holds (encoded per effect verb in SimFacade._mission_target_valid). `interception_modifier` adds (or subtracts) from the base interception percent before the cap. Magnitudes (`amount`, `duration`) live here so the table is the single source of mission tuning. Active missions run from two paths: the alliance-scope espionage screen and a spy unit standing on a foreign city tile. Records with `kind: passive` are the pure-information missions (§25.6): they never run and spend no EP — their intel stays revealed WHILE the attacker's banked EP against the target meets a threshold of intel_mission_cost x threshold_multiplier/100, scaled by the same EP-advantage curve as active costs plus a distance surcharge (intel_passive_distance_percent). `scope` says what a passive record reveals against: the whole target alliance or one specific city. Verbs: see_demographics (rival empire statistics on the espionage advisor); investigate_city (full readout of one city: population, production, structures); see_research (rival's current research and progress); city_visibility (live sight over one city's surroundings, radius intel_city_visibility_radius); detect_missions (missions run against you are attributed to their perpetrator instead of anonymous).
